articles.title: Minnesota lawmakers consider banning prediction markets
articles.summary: Minnesota legislators are weighing a ban on prediction markets, joining the growing state-level backlash against platforms that let users bet on political outcomes and other events. The move signals how quickly the regulatory landscape is shifting after prediction markets' breakout 2024 election year, potentially forcing platforms to navigate a patchwork of state restrictions that could fragment the US market.

articles.title: WATCH: Kalshi founders talk insider trading, gambling, Don Jr. on "The Axios Show"
articles.summary: Kalshi's founders appeared on "The Axios Show" to address controversies around insider trading allegations, gambling regulations, and Donald Trump Jr.'s involvement with the platform. The interview likely signals Kalshi's push to legitimize prediction markets amid regulatory scrutiny and public skepticism about whether these platforms are sophisticated forecasting tools or glorified betting sites.

articles.title: One of Kalshi's fastest-growing markets is about tech layoffs, cofounders say
articles.summary: Kalshi's cofounders report that betting on tech layoffs has become one of their platform's fastest-growing markets. The surge suggests traders see massive profit potential in predicting which companies will cut jobs next, turning corporate misery into a liquid betting opportunity.

articles.title: Prediction markets: Are we betting on the wrong horse?
articles.summary: Prediction markets might be fundamentally flawed in how they assess and price political and economic outcomes. If the core mechanics are broken, billions in betting volume could be systematically mispricing everything from elections to Fed policy decisions.

articles.title: Prediction Markets May Be Coming for ETFs
articles.summary: ETF issuers are exploring ways to integrate prediction market mechanics into exchange-traded funds, potentially creating investment vehicles that bet on specific outcomes or events. This could massively expand prediction markets beyond political races and sports by giving retail investors SEC-regulated access to bet on everything from corporate earnings to climate targets through their regular brokerage accounts.

articles.title: Insider Trading in Prediction Markets Poses Compliance Risks
articles.summary: Prediction markets are wrestling with insider trading scandals as traders with privileged information skew odds and undermine market integrity. Regulators are now eyeing these platforms more closely, which could mean stricter rules and compliance costs that change how prediction markets operate.

articles.title: Kalshi March Madness Volume Blows Past Election Record
articles.summary: Kalshi's March Madness betting volume has shattered the platform's previous record set during the 2024 election cycle. This signals that sports betting might be the real growth engine for prediction markets, not the political drama that gets all the headlines.

articles.title: Newly created Polymarket accounts bet big on US-Iran ceasefire in hours before Trump’s announcement
articles.summary: New Polymarket accounts placed large bets on a US-Iran ceasefire just hours before Trump announced one, suggesting either remarkable timing or potential insider trading. The pattern raises serious questions about information leakage in prediction markets and whether big money consistently flows in ahead of major geopolitical announcements.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Iran Bets Draw Fresh Disputes and Insider Scrutiny
articles.summary: Polymarket faces new controversies over Iran-related betting markets, with questions emerging about potential insider trading and market manipulation. These disputes highlight ongoing concerns about information asymmetries and market integrity that could determine whether prediction platforms maintain credibility with serious money.

articles.title: Polymarket Rebels Still Betting in Banned Countries Using VPNs
articles.summary: Polymarket users in restricted countries are circumventing geographic bans by using VPNs to place bets on the platform. This underground activity shows prediction markets have enough appeal to drive users toward technical workarounds, potentially forcing regulators to either crack down harder or reconsider blanket prohibitions.

articles.title: The big state gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: States are placing massive bets on prediction markets as a revenue source, regulatory framework, or both. If they get it right, we're looking at legitimized mainstream adoption, but if they botch the regulations, it could set the industry back years.

articles.title: House Democrats call on federal regulator to crack down on offshore prediction market war bets
articles.summary: House Democrats are pushing federal regulators to ban or restrict prediction markets that allow betting on wars and military conflicts, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi that operate offshore betting exchanges. The crackdown could signal broader regulatory hostility toward prediction markets, especially those covering sensitive geopolitical events that lawmakers view as inappropriate for wagering.
